analysis.reasoning: Core message: Celebrates International Polar Bear Day, warning about threats (poaching, climate change, poor ecology) and noting that international and national government programs help polar bears survive, with 5,000–7,000 living in Russia. Evaluation: RU1 (West immoral/hostile) – not referenced; no critique of the West or its allies. RU2 (Russia leads alternative system) – no mention of multipolarity or non-Western cooperation. RU3 (Russia is a benevolent partner) – partially relevant: it credits government programs (implicitly including Russia’s) helping the species survive, framing a positive, helpful role; however, it’s general and not explicitly about Russia’s international partnerships or peace/development. RU4 (Russian history admirable) – no historical content. RU5 (Russia at the world’s forefront) – no claim of leadership or superiority. RU6 (Russia is interesting in culture/nature/heritage) – yes: it highlights Russian nature by noting thousands of polar bears living in Russia, promoting Russia’s natural heritage. Conclusion: RU6 clearly applies; RU3 is too indirect to assert confidently.
